title: From GenAI Pilot to Governed AgentOps Control Plane

slides_or_scenes.title: The enterprise problem
slides_or_scenes.message: AI agent pilots are easy to start but hard to govern, evaluate, audit, and scale.

slides_or_scenes.title: The missing layer
slides_or_scenes.message: Enterprises need a control plane between agents, data, tools, models, approvals, and audit.

slides_or_scenes.title: Governance before execution
slides_or_scenes.message: A use case should pass suitability, risk, data, evaluation, approval, and production gates before real action.

slides_or_scenes.title: Policy and runtime boundaries
slides_or_scenes.message: Agent actions are evaluated deterministically before tools, providers, or external channels are used.

slides_or_scenes.title: Evidence and audit
slides_or_scenes.message: Every important decision should be backed by evidence and traceable through an audit event bus.

slides_or_scenes.title: Business proof point
slides_or_scenes.message: Procurement demonstrates PO, invoice, challan, vendor, governance, and sandbox flow without risky live execution.

slides_or_scenes.title: Open-source invitation
slides_or_scenes.message: The project is open, deterministic, local-first, and ready for architecture feedback and contribution.

demo_outcome: Audience understands why this is not a chatbot framework, but an AgentOps control plane.
